competitor_patterns: Long-form content dominates: Trigearlab ranks #1 with 9,735 words; Geeky Gadgets ranks #1 with 4,008 words — our posts average ~1,200 words, roughly 3-8x shorter than top-ranking pages

Structured buyer's guides with explicit evaluation criteria outperform product-first pages — Trigearlab uses a dedicated 'Buyer's Guide' H2 section explaining what to look for before recommending products
Year-tagged titles ('2026', '2024') consistently appear in top-ranking snippets and titles, signalling freshness to both Google and users — all top competitors use year suffixes
Review-format content (has_reviews: true) appears in every top-3 result across both analysed keywords, confirming Google rewards third-party-style credibility signals even on brand-adjacent queries

Top competitors targeting 'best cycling glasses 2026' have FAQ sections and comparison frameworks embedded in content, not as afterthoughts — 100percent.com has FAQ; our posts have FAQs but lack comparison tables in most posts
our_gaps: Word count deficit is severe: our posts are 1,100–1,260 words vs competitor average of 4,000–9,700 words for ranking #1 on 'best cycling glasses' type queries — we lack the depth Google rewards for competitive informational keywords

We have no standalone 'best cycling glasses 2026' or 'best road cycling sunglasses 2026' roundup page — competitors ranking for these terms have dedicated, comprehensive comparison articles; we only have product-specific posts

Comparison tables are used in only 1 of our 4 recent posts (Sigma vs Velluto), but top competitors and our own data show the Sigma post performs well — tables need to be standard across all comparison and roundup content

Our posts are brand-centric from headline to CTA, while top-ranking content leads with the rider's problem or a category-wide review framework before introducing a brand — this reduces our eligibility for 'best of' SERP features

We have no content targeting 'Oakley alternatives' or 'Oakley alternative' (184+51 impressions, positions 7.0–7.0, only 5+2 clicks) despite this being a high-intent comparison query where we have a clear price and feature advantage
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Alibaba ranks #1 (with a 404), meaning the top result is broken; this is an immediate content gap we can own with a dedicated post on the StradaPro's click-in lens system
lightweight cycling sunglasses 2026 — not covered in any recent post despite the 25g frame being our #1 differentiator; competitors do not lead with weight, making this a blue-ocean long-tail angle

road cycling glasses anti-fog — our anti-fog post exists but targets generic anti-fog ventilation; a more specific 'road cycling glasses anti-fog 2026' title and H1 would capture this intent cluster more precisely

Oakley Jawbreaker alternatives 2026 — Trigearlab's #1-ranking post specifically names 'Oakley Jawbreaker — Best Road Cycling Sunglasses' as an H2; a Velluto post targeting this exact query with a direct comparison would intercept high-intent buyers

cycling glasses interchangeable lenses UV400 — a combined feature query that matches our exact product spec (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione + UV400); no competitor content directly targets this three-feature combination
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 2,500 words for any post competing on 'best cycling glasses 2026' or 'Oakley alternatives' — structure as a buyer's guide with an explicit 'How We Evaluated' or 'What to Look For' section before any product recommendation

Lead every post with the rider's pain point in the first 100 words (not the brand name) — mirror Trigearlab's approach of framing the problem before the solution to align with how searchers phrase their queries

Include a comparison table in every post that names at least 2–3 competitor products by name (Oakley Jawbreaker, 100% Speedcraft, Rudy Project) with Velluto StradaPro in the final column — this is what earns featured snippet eligibility for comparison queries

Add a 'Who Should Buy This' and 'Who Should Not Buy This' section to roundup posts — this pattern appears in top-ranking review content and signals balanced, trustworthy editorial judgment to Google's quality algorithms

Target the 'veluto' misspelling (298 impressions, 0.7% CTR, position 5.4) by adding a note in the brand page or a dedicated FAQ entry: 'Looking for Velluto? You may have searched veluto — here's the correct spelling' to capture these near-zero-click impressions

For the 'Oakley alternatives' post, lead with price comparison data — Oakley Jawbreaker retails at €180–220; position StradaPro's Italian design + interchangeable lenses as delivering equivalent performance at a fraction of the cost, with the 30-day trial as the risk-reversal hook

Publish the next post with an exact-match H1 for one of these queries: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026', 'Oakley Alternatives for Road Cyclists 2026', or 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Complete Guide 2026' — our current H1s are too brand-specific to rank for category queries
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table (competitor name | weight | lens system | price | trial policy) to every future post and retrofit it into the top 3 existing posts — tables improve featured snippet eligibility and dwell time simultaneously

Append '| Velluto' to all meta titles and ensure every title contains the year '2026' and a primary keyword in the first 35 characters — current titles like 'Half vs Full Frame Cycling Glasses: Aerodynamics 2026 | Velluto' are well-structured but need the keyword closer to the front for CTR on competitive queries

Implement FAQ schema markup (JSON-LD) on all posts — our posts already have FAQ H2 sections but without structured data markup, we are not eligible for Google's FAQ rich results which would dramatically increase SERP real estate and CTR from positions 5–9
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'veluto' (298 impressions, 0.7% CTR, pos 5.4) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store 2026 | Why: The misspelling query lands on our brand pages; a title that immediately signals 'official' and corrects the spelling builds trust and improves CTR from brand-confused searchers — keep under 60 chars

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (203 impressions, 2.5% CTR, pos 8.8) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested on Real Rides | Why: 'Tested on real rides' directly references the 30-day trial differentiator and creates a curiosity gap that outperforms generic 'top 10' titles at position 8–9 where CTR is already low

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (184 impressions, 2.7% CTR, pos 7.0) → New title: Oakley Alternatives 2026: Lighter, Cheaper & Trial Period | Why: Leads with the three most compelling switching motivators (weight, price, risk-free trial) directly in the title — functional over clever for a high-intent comparison query
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.5, 141 impressions, 6 clicks) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 7 Pairs Tested by Road Cyclists' | Add: Expand to 2,500+ words with a structured comparison table of 6–7 named competitors + StradaPro, an explicit 'How We Tested' section, weight and lens-swap benchmarks, and FAQ schema — this mirrors the Trigearlab format that ranks #1 for the equivalent query

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.0, 184 impressions, 5 clicks) → Topic: 'Oakley Jawbreaker Alternatives 2026: 5 Road Cycling Options That Cost Less' | Add: Name Oakley Jawbreaker, 100% Speedcraft, Rudy Project Cutline as comparators; build a table with price, weight, lens system, UV rating, and trial policy columns; place StradaPro's 25g weight and 30-day trial as the closing argument — exact-match H1 on 'Oakley alternatives'

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 8.8, 203 impressions, 5 clicks) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Ranked by Weight, Lens System & Value' | Add: A ranked list format (not just a buyer's guide) with explicit position numbers (#1, #2, etc.), a scoring table, and a 'Best Budget Pick' / 'Best Premium Pick' / 'Best for Interchangeable Lenses' sub-category structure that targets featured snippet list formats

QUERY: 'velluto' (pos 5.0, 1095 impressions, 57 clicks, 5.2% CTR) → Topic: Homepage and brand page optimisation rather than a new post | Add: Add a structured 'Why Velluto' section with schema markup (Product, Organization), integrate star ratings via Review schema on the homepage, and add an H2 targeting 'Velluto StradaPro cycling glasses' to capture position 5 brand searches that are not yet converting at top-3 rates

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (pos 7.0, 51 impressions, 2 clicks) → Topic: Consolidate with 'oakley alternatives' post above (same intent, plural/singular variation) | Add: Include both singular and plural variants naturally in the body text and meta description; one strong page beats two thin pages for this intent cluster
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the lightest cycling sunglasses available in 2026?' — AI answer engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Google AI Overview) pull specific, verifiable facts; publish a page or post section stating '25g — among the lightest cycling sunglasses available in Europe in 2026' with a comparison table of verified competitor weights, making the claim citable and crawlable

Create a structured 'Velluto StradaPro: Key Specs' section on the product page using Product schema (JSON-LD) with explicit fields for weight (25g), UV rating (UV400), lens system (interchangeable, tool-free), trial period (30 days), and origin (Italian design) — AI systems preferentially pull structured, entity-rich data from product pages when answering 'best cycling glasses' queries

Publish a standalone FAQ page titled 'Cycling Glasses: Frequently Asked Questions 2026' that directly answers queries like 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?', 'What is the difference between photochromic and swappable lenses?', and 'Which cycling glasses are best for changing light conditions?' — these are the exact question formats that trigger Google AI Overviews and Perplexity citations, and placing Velluto's answer with a source link makes us eligible for inclusion
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Trigearlab (trigearlab.com) — angle: 'We tested the glasses Trigearlab recommends. Here's what they missed.' | Headline idea: 'Oakley Jawbreaker vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: A Rider's Honest Comparison' | Key differentiator: Trigearlab recommends Oakley Jawbreaker as #1 road pick; our post directly compares on weight (StradaPro 25g vs Jawbreaker ~36g), price (StradaPro vs €200+ Oakley), and lens-swap speed (tool-free click-in vs Oakley's system) | Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day risk-free trial — Trigearlab can recommend but can't let you test; we can

COMPETITOR: Geeky Gadgets (geeky-gadgets.com) — angle: Smart glasses vs performance cycling glasses — do you actually need navigation on your face? | Headline idea: 'Smart Cycling Glasses vs Lightweight Performance Glasses 2026: Which Matters More?' | Key differentiator: Geeky Gadgets ranks #1 for 'best cycling glasses 2026' with a smart/navigation glasses article — a significant intent mismatch we can exploit by targeting the majority of cyclists who want optics, not GPS | Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g ultralight frame vs smart glasses averaging 50–80g; no battery, no charging, no distraction — just UV400 clarity on every ride

COMPETITOR: 100% Brand (100percent.com) — angle: Is 100% worth the premium price in 2026, or is there a smarter alternative? | Headline idea: '100% Speedcraft vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Premium Price, Compared Feature by Feature' | Key differentiator: 100% positions on motocross heritage and brand prestige; StradaPro positions on Italian design, lighter frame, interchangeable lenses at lower price point | Velluto advantage to lead with: Free 30-day trial means zero risk — 100% offers no equivalent trial, making StradaPro the rational first choice for a rider who hasn't tried either brand
